message:
Fix reference-style Markdown links in operator descriptions

The ReferenceCard component only supports inline-style links [text](url),
not reference-style links [text][ref]. Replace unresolved reference links
with plain text in operator descriptions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -420,7 +420,7 @@ operators:
     example: 'load_s3 "s3://my-bucket/obj.csv"'
     path: 'reference/operators/load_s3'
   - name: 'load_sqs'
-    description: 'Loads bytes from [Amazon SQS][sqs] queues.'
+    description: 'Loads bytes from Amazon SQS queues.'
     example: 'load_sqs "sqs://tenzir"'
     path: 'reference/operators/load_sqs'
   - name: 'load_stdin'
@@ -564,7 +564,7 @@ operators:
     example: 'read_kv r"(\s+)[A-Z_]+:", r":\s*"'
     path: 'reference/operators/read_kv'
   - name: 'read_leef'
-    description: 'Parses an incoming [LEEF][leef] stream into events.'
+    description: 'Parses an incoming LEEF stream into events.'
     example: 'read_leef'
     path: 'reference/operators/read_leef'
   - name: 'read_lines'
@@ -588,7 +588,7 @@ operators:
     example: 'read_ssv header="name count"'
     path: 'reference/operators/read_ssv'
   - name: 'read_suricata'
-    description: 'Parse an incoming [Suricata EVE JSON][eve-json] stream into events.'
+    description: 'Parses an incoming Suricata EVE JSON stream into events.'
     example: 'read_suricata'
     path: 'reference/operators/read_suricata'
   - name: 'read_syslog'
@@ -656,7 +656,7 @@ operators:
     example: 'save_s3 "s3://my-bucket/obj.csv"'
     path: 'reference/operators/save_s3'
   - name: 'save_sqs'
-    description: 'Saves bytes to [Amazon SQS][sqs] queues.'
+    description: 'Saves bytes to Amazon SQS queues.'
     example: 'save_sqs "sqs://tenzir"'
     path: 'reference/operators/save_sqs'
   - name: 'save_stdout'
@@ -736,7 +736,7 @@ operators:
     example: 'to_snowflake account_identifier="…'
     path: 'reference/operators/to_snowflake'
   - name: 'to_splunk'
-    description: 'Sends events to a Splunk [HTTP Event Collector (HEC)][hec].'
+    description: 'Sends events to a Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC).'
     example: 'to_splunk "localhost:8088", …'
     path: 'reference/operators/to_splunk'
   - name: 'write_bitz'
@@ -1698,7 +1698,7 @@ read_kv r"(\s+)[A-Z_]+:", r":\s*"
 
 </ReferenceCard>
 
-<ReferenceCard title="read_leef" description="Parses an incoming [LEEF][leef] stream into events." href="/reference/operators/read_leef">
+<ReferenceCard title="read_leef" description="Parses an incoming LEEF stream into events." href="/reference/operators/read_leef">
 
 ```tql
 read_leef
@@ -1746,7 +1746,7 @@ read_ssv header="name count"
 
 </ReferenceCard>
 
-<ReferenceCard title="read_suricata" description="Parse an incoming [Suricata EVE JSON][eve-json] stream into events." href="/reference/operators/read_suricata">
+<ReferenceCard title="read_suricata" description="Parses an incoming Suricata EVE JSON stream into events." href="/reference/operators/read_suricata">
 
 ```tql
 read_suricata
@@ -2062,7 +2062,7 @@ load_s3 "s3://my-bucket/obj.csv"
 
 </ReferenceCard>
 
-<ReferenceCard title="load_sqs" description="Loads bytes from [Amazon SQS][sqs] queues." href="/reference/operators/load_sqs">
+<ReferenceCard title="load_sqs" description="Loads bytes from Amazon SQS queues." href="/reference/operators/load_sqs">
 
 ```tql
 load_sqs "sqs://tenzir"
@@ -2392,7 +2392,7 @@ save_s3 "s3://my-bucket/obj.csv"
 
 </ReferenceCard>
 
-<ReferenceCard title="save_sqs" description="Saves bytes to [Amazon SQS][sqs] queues." href="/reference/operators/save_sqs">
+<ReferenceCard title="save_sqs" description="Saves bytes to Amazon SQS queues." href="/reference/operators/save_sqs">
 
 ```tql

 </ReferenceCard>
 
-<ReferenceCard title="to_splunk" description="Sends events to a Splunk [HTTP Event Collector (HEC)][hec]." href="/reference/operators/to_splunk">
+<ReferenceCard title="to_splunk" description="Sends events to a Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC)." href="/reference/operators/to_splunk">
